evidences.title: NFL Draft 2026 Rules Explained: Eligibility, Picks, Contracts
evidences.content: ## How the Draft Order is Set Each of the 32 clubs gets one pick in each of the seven rounds before compensatory selections are added. The basic order is the reverse of the previous season’s finish: the worst team picks first, the Super Bowl champion picks last in each round, absent trades. The NFL breaks the order down into two buckets. Non-playoff teams receive slots 1-20, ordered by regular-season standing. Playoff teams receive slots 21-32, ordered by how far they advanced, with ties among teams exiting in the same round broken by regular-season record. The Super Bowl loser picks 31st and the champion 32nd. [...] The league’s formula is not fully public. But the NFL does say it is based on three core factors: salary, playing time, and postseason honors. Not every departing or incoming free agent counts. The formula applies to a defined class of players called compensatory free agents, or CFAs. The practical rules are: A team must suffer a net loss of CFAs to qualify for standard compensatory picks. Picks are awarded from Round 3 through Round 7 based on the value of the lost players. No team may receive more than four compensatory picks in a single year. A team that loses an...

evidences.title: Super Bowl LX: 5 factors that will determine New England Patriots vs. Seattle Seahawks
evidences.content: New England has more options when it comes to answering a question like this because so many players have stepped up throughout the season. Running back TreVeyon Henderson hasn't gotten as much run lately -- he had three carries against Denver -- but he gained 911 rushing yards as a rookie and produced four runs of 50-plus yards. Wide receiver Kayshon Boutte leads the team in receiving yards in the postseason, while Marcus Jones has returned two punts and two interceptions for touchdowns this year (including playoffs). It wouldn't be surprising if any one of those players did something in this game that shifted momentum in a major way. Both teams reached the Super Bowl by relying on more than their stars in key moments. It makes perfect sense to think that's how one of them will end up [...] It's difficult to imagine Darnold doing more than he just did in his team's NFC championship win over the Rams. The same guy who had been in game-manager mode for the second half of the regular season -- he hadn't thrown for more than 300 yards since a Week 9 win over Washington -- overwhelmed the Rams defense in his best performance of the 2025 campaign.
